concerns.text: FR-001 (accept list of URLs) is not referenced by any User Story; no story cites this requirement, breaking traceability between functional requirements and user scenarios.
FR-002 (automatic extraction) is orphaned; no User Story lists FR-002 as an anchored requirement, leaving the requirement unlinked to functional intent.
FR-003 (reconstruct p‑value) is not anchored to any User Story, creating a gap between the stated capability and the user‑level goals.
SC-002 (inconsistency‑detection precision ≥ 90 %) is not tied to any User Story; the success criterion lacks an explicit story anchor, making its relevance unclear.
SC-003 (statistical tests must be computed with SciPy) is not referenced by any User Story, violating the required traceability between success criteria and user scenarios.

FR-004 criterion 1 mentions a confidence level as “[deferred]” while also using a fixed 0.05 threshold; the lack of a concrete confidence level (e.g., 95 %) makes the rule ambiguous and non‑operational.

FR-005 calls for a Wilson “[deferred]” confidence interval without specifying the confidence level, rendering the interval undefined; additionally, it lists statistical analyses (chi‑squared goodness‑of‑fit, Cochran‑Armitage trend test, Bonferroni‑adjusted subgroup tests) that are not mentioned in the Idea document, constituting an undocumented scope expansion.
